title: InvocationContracts
description: Typed contracts for all inter-agent communication: SkillInvokeRequest, SkillInvokeResult, and HandoffEnvelope. All payloads MUST carry trace_id for distributed tracing.

trace_id.description: Must match the GlobalState.trace_id for the current execution. Enables distributed tracing.

skill.description: Must be a skill listed in mappings/skills-map.yaml for this agent_id. RBAC will deny unmapped skills.

constraints.description: Hard constraints the skill implementation must honor (e.g., 'Use non-interactive flags', 'Output must be JSON serializable').

timeout_seconds.description: REQUIRED. Strict maximum execution time (seconds) for this skill invocation. On breach, the agent must fire circuit breaker and return a failed HandoffEnvelope. Per-category budgets: install=120, build=180, test=300, migrate=60, scan=90, probe=30.

require_non_interactive.description: REQUIRED. MUST be true whenever executing CLI tools to prevent Y/N prompt hangs. Only false for pure in-memory or text-generation operations.

retry_policy.description: Exponential backoff retry configuration. Inspired by LangGraph ToolNode retry policy.

max_attempts.description: Maximum retry attempts including the initial execution. When exhausted, circuit breaker fires.

backoff_seconds.description: Initial wait in seconds before first retry. Doubles on each subsequent attempt (2s → 4s → STOP).

retry_on_timeout.description: If false (default), timeout failures immediately fire circuit breaker without retry. If true, timeout failures are retried up to max_attempts.

output_log_file.description: Optional. File path where stdout/stderr should be redirected (e.g., '{trace_id}_build.log'). Prevents context-buffer overflow. Agent should read only tail-50 after completion.

status.description: 'timeout' indicates the operation was killed by the timeout_seconds limit — circuit breaker logic applies.

duration_seconds.description: Actual elapsed execution time. Used by A00 to audit against timeout_seconds budget.

artifacts.description: File paths or URIs to generated artifacts. Must be referenced in GlobalState.artifacts_map.

risk_flags.description: Any observed risks, warnings, or partial failures that did not block completion but warrant A00's attention.

log_tail.description: Last 50 lines of the operation log (if output_log_file was used). Inlined for agent context without overflowing.

to.description: Target agent, 'User' for HITL escalation, or 'End' for lifecycle termination.

trace_id.description: Must match GlobalState.trace_id. Required for distributed tracing continuity.

reason.description: Human-readable reason for the handoff (e.g., 'Backend task cards ready for quality gate', 'TIMEOUT: npm build exceeded 180s limit').

handoff_type.description: Categorizes why this handoff is occurring. Enables A00 to apply correct routing logic automatically.

context.description: Structured context relevant to the target agent. Include artifact paths, task IDs, relevant error details.

blocking_issues.description: List of explicit blockers preventing progress. REQUIRED non-empty when handoff_type is 'circuit_breaker', 'gate_fail', or 'hitl_request'.

patch_strategy.description: MetaGPT-style reflexion: when circuit_breaker or reflexion_retry, this field MUST describe root cause + proposed fix before the implementer agent retries.

expected_decision.description: What decision or action the target agent is expected to make (e.g., 'approve_and_route_to_implementation', 'fix_rate_limit_vulnerability', 'confirm_deployment').

state_patch.description: LangGraph-style reducer patch: partial GlobalState fields this agent wants to mutate (e.g., artifacts_map updates). A00 applies after reviewing.
